prompt: A long cathedral corridor or palace hallway photographed in deep perspective, shot from ground level looking toward a single warm light source at the far end. The light source — a chandelier or lantern — fully illuminates the arches and columns nearest to it at the far end. Moving toward the camera, each successive archway receives progressively less light, growing dimmer and dimmer. The three or four arches closest to the camera are almost entirely in darkness — the stone columns and vaulted ceilings are barely visible, only their silhouettes remain. The transition from full illumination to near-total darkness is the entire subject of the image. The architecture is grand and intact throughout — nothing is broken or collapsed. Only the light fails to reach. Shot as a long-exposure architectural photograph, slightly desaturated, stone and shadow. No people, no text, no labels, no overlays. Negative prompt: text, labels, diagrams, arrows, people, graphs, equations, modern furniture, bright colors, HDR, overexposed
metaphor: Cathedral arches fade into darkness as they recede from a distant light, mirroring how signals weaken across deeper layers of a network.
